layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE LAYOUT. The slide is structured as a single dominant table container spanning the full usable slide area below the title. The table uses a multi-column grid structure with a fixed left column for workstream names and details, a middle column for owner assignments, and four right columns representing timeline quarters (2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, and 2027). The layout follows a formal regulatory table pattern (Flat Geometry Table, Section 4 Option C) with no filled header bars, using accent tabs beneath column headers and dashed grey separator lines between rows. Content is distributed evenly across the vertical space with all rows sharing identical heights to maintain horizontal alignment. The table fills the slide confidently with minimal padding, appropriate for dense structured content. A thin brand-color border wraps the entire table container with subtle rounded corners and an even light grey shadow glow for card lift.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.1, 0.9]

Containers:
  C0 (SLIDE TITLE AREA): Top-aligned title area spanning full slide width. Contains the main slide heading 'Fund Accounting platform initiative workplan' in heading font, brand primary color, left-aligned. Height constrained to 10% of slide area maximum. Clear vertical gap below the title before the table container begins. No background fill, no border. The yellow annotation box visible in the source slide is removed entirely — it is editorial markup, not content.
  C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE): Dominant table container filling all remaining slide space below the title with a small top margin. Structured as a Flat Geometry Table (Design Guidelines Section 4 Option C) — formal, regulatory-appropriate pattern with no filled header bars. The table uses a flex row structure with seven columns: (1) Workstream name column (narrow, 12% width), (2) Details column (wider, 18% width), (3) Owner(s) column (medium, 15% width), (4-7) Four timeline columns for 2026 Q2, Q3, Q4, and 2027 (equal-peers, sharing remaining 55% width split 4 ways, approximately 13.75% each). All columns are flex columns internally. HEADER ROW: Each column header is plain bold text in body font, brand dark color, left-aligned with small left inset. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary fill, very thin height, narrow width relative to column, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned, positioned fully below the text without overlap). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line (brand dark color) spanning that column's width with small white gutters between columns (not a single full-width line). CONTENT ROWS: The table contains five major workstream rows (i. Conduct discovery, ii. Select and onboard partner, iii. Implement fund accounting platform – 3rd-party funds/BDC, iv. Implement fund accounting platform - Holdings, v. Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments). Each workstream row is subdivided into task rows based on the Details column content. All task rows within the table share identical height via equal flexGrow values to maintain perfect horizontal alignment across all columns. ROW SEPARATORS: Thin dashed grey horizontal lines between every task row, spanning full table width, positioned at row boundaries. No separator after the final row. WORKSTREAM COLUMN: Workstream names (i., ii., iii., iv., v.) are bold, brand dark color, left-aligned with small left inset, vertically centered within their merged row span. DETAILS COLUMN: Each task detail is plain body text, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset, vertically centered within its row. OWNER(S) COLUMN: Owner assignments use two-part structure: role prefix (Finance/Ops:, Tech:, TO:) in bold brand dark color, followed by names in regular weight brand text color. Multiple names separated by commas. Text left-aligned with small left inset, vertically top-aligned within the row to accommodate multi-line content. TIMELINE COLUMNS (Q2, Q3, Q4, 2027): Deliverable text for each task, body font, brand text color, left-aligned with small left inset, vertically top-aligned within the row. Bullet points rendered as native PowerPoint bullets with brand-color bullet markers. Rows with no deliverable content for a given quarter remain empty (white fill, no text). CONTAINER STYLING: White fill, thin brand primary border, visibly rounded corners (subtle, not sharp), even light grey shadow glow for card lift effect. Internal padding: minimal on all sides (dense content requires tight padding per Beauty Principle 0.1). The table confidently fills the available slide space. FONT SIZING: All body text within the table uses the same font size (within-slide uniformity, Rule C6). Font size target range: small-to-medium, optimized to fit dense multi-line cell content (some cells contain 3-4 bullet points) without overflow. Column headers slightly larger or same size as body, bold weight. ALIGNMENT PRECISION: All task rows across all seven columns share identical top_pct and height_pct values. Dashed separator lines appear at identical vertical positions across all columns. This is a hard structural constraint (Blueprint Step 0.5c). CONTENT VOLUME ACCOMMODATION: The table contains approximately 15-20 task rows total across five workstreams. Vertical space is distributed evenly across all rows. The rightmost timeline columns contain the densest content (multi-line bullet lists), requiring adequate row height to prevent text clipping. The layout prioritizes readability and horizontal alignment over generous whitespace.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide is well-filled by the dominant table container. No bottom decorative layer is needed. Adding one would violate the accent color budget and provide no visual grouping benefit.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide contains no cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements. All content is self-contained within the table structure.

_reference_violations.description: C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE): The table is missing the fifth workstream row 'v. Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments'. Source slide shows 5 workstreams (i through v), but the sketch only renders 4 visible workstreams (i through iv). The fifth workstream and its associated task rows are absent.

C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE): Multiple task rows are missing. Source slide contains approximately 15-20 task rows across 5 workstreams. The sketch appears to have fewer rows, particularly missing all task rows associated with workstream v and potentially some rows from other workstreams.

C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE): Severe text overflow and clipping visible in multiple cells. The 2026 Q2 column shows heavily overlapping text that is illegible. Multiple cells in the Owner(s) column and timeline columns show text that overflows cell boundaries or overlaps with adjacent text, making content unreadable.

C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE): Font size is too small for the available vertical space in table rows. Many rows appear to have excessive white space below the text content, indicating the text block occupies less than 65% of the row height. This is particularly visible in single-line cells where the row height could accommodate much larger text.

_reference_critique:
C1 (FULL-WIDTH WORKSTREAM TABLE) has multiple critical content fidelity and rendering failures:

1. MISSING WORKSTREAM (CRITICAL): The fifth workstream "v. Implement fund accounting platform – non-loan investments" and all its associated task rows are completely absent from the sketch. The source slide clearly shows 5 workstreams (i, ii, iii, iv, v), but the sketch only renders 4. SVGSketcher must add the missing workstream v with all its task rows to match the source data structure.

2. MISSING TASK ROWS (CRITICAL): The total row count is significantly lower than the source slide's 15-20 task rows. SVGSketcher must verify that every task row from the source slide is present in the sketch, particularly ensuring all rows for workstream v are included, and that no rows from workstreams i-iv are omitted.

3. TEXT OVERFLOW AND CLIPPING (CRITICAL): Severe text overflow is visible in multiple cells, particularly in the 2026 Q2 column where text is completely illegible due to overlapping lines. The Owner(s) column also shows text clipping. SVGSketcher must reduce the font size for all table body text from its current value to a smaller size (likely 9-10pt range) to ensure all multi-line cell content fits within cell boundaries without overflow or clipping. Apply this reduced font size uniformly to all body text cells (within-slide uniformity C6).

4. FONT SIZE TOO SMALL FOR ROW HEIGHT (MAJOR): Despite the overflow issues in dense cells, many single-line cells show excessive white space, indicating poor font size optimization. After fixing the overflow by reducing font size, SVGSketcher should then increase row heights proportionally to better fill the available vertical space while maintaining the smaller font size needed to prevent overflow in dense cells.

SVGSketcher must: (a) add all missing workstream v content, (b) verify all task rows from source are present, (c) reduce body text font size to 9-10pt to eliminate all overflow, (d) recalculate row heights to distribute vertical space evenly across the now-complete row set.
